-- 1 of 5 --

Your Pre-Prelaunch, Part 3
!
I just walked you through the shot across the bow and sending people to a survey
ahead of your launch. Now, I just want to show you some of the results that I got.
This was with the training launch I did and again this is, if you’re not a trading geek,
none of this probably makes any sense to you.
This stuff was really, really valuable in figuring out what the objections are, what the
hot points are, what got people excited. If you look where you just got some fantastic
responses from, again it’s because you’re in their pre-prelaunch before anything’s for
sale, you don’t set up the BS detector and you have people just giving you great
feedback.
This is the kind of feedback we got with a testimonial cruncher. If you look, there are
some great comments, and some very good testimonials considering this is a new
product. We actually got testimonials about our work that we publish in our newsletter
and we’ve got some great testimonials. That’s the testimonial cruncher in action.
Let's talk about identifying the offer, the story and the objections piece and I
want to talk about my next favorite “beyond surveys” email.
One of the most effective things you can do is just ask for a comment, and we’re going
to show you exact wording that I use to get some great response.
The other thing to do as you’re sending out emails is always be watching your stats. If
you watch the open rate, you'll see what different subject lines/content get the click
through.
Specifically the replies you get and how many replies you get will start to give you an
overall feel through for the type of things that your list responds to.
The first example to show you is one that I call the “generic email query”. It's very
simple but incredibly powerful.
Lots of times, once you start building a list especially once a list gets larger for some
reason, it can be tough to get interaction. It can be tough to get people to write back to
you, to answer a query.
What I call the generic email query, I’ve used in lots of different market very, very
effectively. It’s great for getting a big response even if it’s a list that doesn’t normally
respond to you.
“Finally I have a question for you, if you can have a private conversation with me, what
two questions would you like to ask me. Then you say send me your questions at...”.
Then give them your email address to make sure it’s a private looking email address
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module4v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 5 --

that they think is going directly to you. In this case it does go directly to me when I
have the separate query.
“That’s all for now. Look for your next issue very soon” and then this is the magic
paragraph down here.
“Yes, I’m really serious go ahead and push the reply button now and tell me your
question. What do you need help with?”
Specifically what you are doing is telling them you’re really serious and you actually
want them to push the reply button and respond. This gets the responses. If you do
something like this you will get triple or quadruple the responses. It looks simple but
don’t overlook just how powerful this generic email response request is.
Next I want to show you how I tested a prelaunch idea via email.
This is actually for the launch of Product Launch Formula 3.0.
I did this maybe four to six weeks ahead of the launch. I had this idea that I would
start off the launch with a piece of content about the really big product launches. Now,
I’ve always published about for the little guys and how they did really well, with Product
Launch Formula. Frankly those are the people I like to work with, the John Gallagher
and the Andrew Jones, the people who are in offbeat niches that no one ever heard of.
Maybe they were working for a living or beginners and ended up doing Product Llaunch
Formula and did a great launch. I love those launches, I love those stories, I love to
feature those people in our marketing. I decided for 3.0, I wanted to do something just
a little bit different going into this launch.
I thought maybe I’d do a video about the people, like the guru launches. I have never
featured any of the guru launches. I’ve never thought about them, never read case
studies about them, so I thought it might be interesting to do some prelaunch content
about the big million dollar launches.
It seemed like it was a big departure from what I did before, I wanted to test see if the
idea resonated. If people hear about those, the big launches and so I put out this
email.
It talks about a huge misconception, holding a lot of people back. How I was recently
interviewed and one of the questions was whether product launches were still effective
with all the noise in the market.
I start up this idea where there’s rumors going around that product launches don’t work
anymore. This actually was the truth. There were people out there in bolt and bars
talking about launches not working.

-- 3 of 5 --

I wrote a fairly lengthy email about this, and about how launches are working. I kept on
going down here, I started talking about the big guru launches and how I tend to know
the results of them.
Down here is something that I’ve actually never talked about publicly before in the past
when I talk about product launch results.
I’ve always talked about Product Launch Formula Owners in all kinds of different niches.
Down here how I‘ve never talked about the guru launches, then I talk about being able
to see inside of all these guru launches because either they are friends of mine, or they
might be launches I’m helping out on. Or one of my product launch managers is helping
out on this.
This basically sets up for a couple paragraphs down, where I pass this idea that I had
for the prelaunch content. I said, “I’ve never revealed the behind the scene stuff about
the big guru launches before. I told you why I’ve never done it but I’m starting to think
that maybe I should open the kimono a little bit, if you would like to see me put
together a free report or a video about the big guru launches, then write back and let
me know. Just hit your reply button and send me an email. I’ll actually read each reply
if there’s enough interest then I’ll put something together.”
Here I’m basically using a variation where I’m giving them very explicit instructions and
telling them I’ll read their emails.
Write back and let me know, just hit your reply button and send me an email. I’ll
actually read each reply. That type of copy is very, very effective and then I come back
with the PS. I’m really serious about this, if you want me to put together some type of
report with the behind the scenes about the big guru launches and how they’re able to
pull them off, then write back to me. Just hit your reply button and send me an email to
let me know.”
Again the reason I did this is I wanted to test this idea whether there was much interest
in the guru launches, in the great big launches in the internet marketing space.
The interesting thing here was, I got back literally hundreds and hundreds of responses
where people just wrote back to me and told me they were very much interested.
I’d say their responses were at least 99% in favor of, yes, tell me about it as opposed to
every, just a few emails that wrote back and said I don’t care about the gurus. Forget
about it. I’m speaking here about the gurus. Very, very strong response, probably the
biggest response I’ve ever gotten to anything in the internet marketing which in terms
at responding to an email query like this, just hundreds and hundreds and hundreds of
responses.

That’s how I pre-tested it, the interest in this launch a story idea. I did it to start off
talking about the big launches instead of the little launches.
